userStories.title: Harden koda_token cookie security attributes
userStories.description: Add `secure: true`, `sameSite: 'strict'`, and `maxAge: 604800` options to the `useCookie('koda_token')` call in `apps/web/composables/useAuth.ts` line 33. The `httpOnly` option must NOT be set to true because `useApi.ts` reads `auth.token.value` client-side to construct the Authorization header — this is a documented trade-off. All existing auth flows (login, register, logout, fetchUser) must continue to function correctly against the same cookie ref.
acceptanceCriteria: When useAuth() initializes the token cookie, useCookie is called with a second argument containing `secure: true`
When useAuth() initializes the token cookie, useCookie is called with a second argument containing `sameSite: 'strict'`
When useAuth() initializes the token cookie, useCookie is called with a second argument containing `maxAge: 604800`
When login() resolves successfully, token.value equals the accessToken string from the API response
When logout() is called, token.value is set to null
Given token.value is non-null, when fetchUser() is called and the API responds successfully, user.value is set to the returned AuthUser and fetchUser() returns true
Given token.value is null, when fetchUser() is called, it returns false without making any network request

userStories.title: Fix CommentThread form validation with toTypedSchema
userStories.description: In `apps/web/components/CommentThread.vue`, the `commentSchema` z.object (lines 44-47) is passed directly to `useForm({ validationSchema })` without being wrapped with `toTypedSchema()` from `@vee-validate/zod`. This silently disables all client-side validation — the Zod constraints (min(1) on body, z.enum on type) are never enforced. Add the import and wrap the schema exactly as done in `CreateTicketDialog.vue` (line 98) and `CreateProjectDialog.vue` (line 69).
acceptanceCriteria: When a user submits the comment form with an empty body field, form validation fires and the body field error message is displayed without calling $api.post
When a user submits the comment form with a body value that passes z.string().min(1), $api.post is called with the body and type values
When a user submits the comment form with a type value not in ['GENERAL','VERIFICATION','FIX_REPORT','REVIEW'], form validation fires and the type field error is displayed without calling $api.post
Given a successful comment submission, resetForm() clears the body field to '' and resets the type field to 'GENERAL'

userStories.title: Reset dialog forms after successful submission
userStories.description: Both `CreateTicketDialog.vue` and `CreateProjectDialog.vue` fail to call `resetForm()` after a successful create, so stale field values appear when the dialog is reopened. In `CreateTicketDialog.vue` (line 109), `resetForm` is not destructured from `useForm()` and is not called. In `CreateProjectDialog.vue` (line 81), `resetForm` is also not destructured (though `handleSubmit`, `setFieldValue`, `isSubmitting`, and `values` are). Both files need `resetForm` destructured and called after `emit('created')` and `emit('update:open', false)` in the success path.
acceptanceCriteria: When CreateTicketDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the title field returns to ''
When CreateTicketDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the type field returns to ''
When CreateTicketDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the priority field returns to 'MEDIUM'
When CreateTicketDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the description field returns to ''
When CreateProjectDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the name field returns to ''
When CreateProjectDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the slug field returns to ''
When CreateProjectDialog's onSubmit() succeeds, resetForm() is called and the key field returns to ''

userStories.title: Use extractApiError in all component error catch blocks
userStories.description:
Five components use the inferior `error instanceof Error ? error.message : fallbackString` pattern that loses structured ApiError details (field-level validation errors, non-zero ret codes). Replace all occurrences with `extractApiError(error)` from `~/composables/useApi`, which correctly handles ApiError.firstError, ofetch FetchError .data.message, and generic Error.message. Additionally, remove the two `console.error` calls in `labels.vue` (lines 45 and 56) — they violate the project's no-console-log policy and are replaced by the toast calls.

Files to update:
- `TicketActionPanel.vue` line 52-54 (performAction catch)
- `CreateTicketDialog.vue` lines 127-129 (onSubmit catch)
- `CreateProjectDialog.vue` lines 116-118 (onSubmit catch)
- `CommentThread.vue` lines 67-70 (onSubmit catch)
- `labels.vue` lines 44-47 and 55-57 (two catch blocks)
acceptanceCriteria: When TicketActionPanel's performAction() catches an ApiError, toast.error is called with the ApiError's firstError string (not a generic fallback)
When CreateTicketDialog's onSubmit() catches an ApiError with field-level errors, toast.error is called with the first field error string via extractApiError
When CreateProjectDialog's onSubmit() catches an ApiError with field-level errors, toast.error is called with the first field error string via extractApiError
When CommentThread's onSubmit() catches an ApiError, toast.error is called with extractApiError(err) rather than err.message
When labels.vue's onSubmit() catches a label creation error, toast.error is called with extractApiError(err) and console.error is not called
When labels.vue's deleteLabel() catches a delete error, toast.error is called with extractApiError(err) and console.error is not called
Given a caught generic Error (not ApiError), extractApiError returns error.message so toast.error displays the plain message

userStories.title: Fix LanguageSwitcher active locale comparison
userStories.description:
In `apps/web/components/LanguageSwitcher.vue`, `locale` from `useI18n()` is a `Ref<string>`. Two bindings compare or pass the Ref object instead of its unwrapped value:
1. Line 31: `locale === loc.code` — comparing a Ref to a string always returns false, so the active button class is never applied
2. Line 42: `:model-value="locale"` — passes the Ref object to the Select component instead of the string value, breaking the dropdown selection

Fix by changing both to use `locale.value`. The `currentLocaleName` computed (line 12) and `switchLocale` function (line 18) are already correct and must not be changed.
acceptanceCriteria: When the active locale code matches a toggle button's loc.code, the button receives the class 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-medium' (locale.value === loc.code evaluates to true)
When the active locale code does not match a toggle button's loc.code, the button receives the class 'text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:bg-muted'
When rendering with 3 or more locales, the Select component's model-value prop equals the string value of locale.value, not the Ref object
When switchLocale(code) is called with a locale code string, setLocale receives that same string code

analysis:
Five isolated bug fixes in apps/web/. All changes are client-side only; no API, CLI, or Prisma changes required.

**US-001 — Cookie hardening (useAuth.ts:33):** The `useCookie('koda_token')` call at line 33 has no security options. Must add `secure: true`, `sameSite: 'strict' as const`, and `maxAge: 604800`. `httpOnly` CANNOT be true because `useApi.ts` reads `auth.token.value` at lines 103-105 to set the Authorization header client-side — this is an intentional trade-off documented in the spec. The cookie ref is shared across `login()` (sets `token.value`), `logout()` (clears to null), and `fetchUser()` (reads for /auth/me header). Security override mandates three-session-tdd.

**US-002 — toTypedSchema (CommentThread.vue:44-55):** The `commentSchema` z.object is passed directly to `useForm({ validationSchema })` without being wrapped in `toTypedSchema()` from `@vee-validate/zod`. This silently disables all client-side validation. The fix is a single import + wrapping the schema. `CreateTicketDialog.vue` (line 98) and `CreateProjectDialog.vue` (line 69) already use `toTypedSchema` correctly and serve as reference implementations.

**US-003 — Dialog form reset (CreateTicketDialog.vue, CreateProjectDialog.vue):** `CreateTicketDialog.vue` at line 109 destructures only `handleSubmit` and `isSubmitting` from `useForm()` — `resetForm` is not destructured and not called after success (lines 124-126). `CreateProjectDialog.vue` at line 81 destructures `handleSubmit, setFieldValue, isSubmitting, values` but omits `resetForm`, which is also not called after success (lines 113-115). Both need `resetForm()` called after `emit('created')` and `emit('update:open', false)`. Note: CreateProjectDialog has watchers on `values.name` and `values.key` — these will continue to function correctly after `resetForm()` resets to initial values `{ name: '', slug: '', key: '' }`.

**US-004 — extractApiError (5 files):** `extractApiError` is already exported from `apps/web/composables/useApi.ts` (lines 68-89). It handles ApiError (returns `firstError`), ofetch FetchError (extracts `.data.message` or `.data.errors`), and generic Error. Four components still use the inferior `error instanceof Error ? error.message : fallback` pattern: TicketActionPanel.vue:53, CreateTicketDialog.vue:128, CreateProjectDialog.vue:117, CommentThread.vue:68. Additionally labels.vue has two `console.error()` calls (lines 45, 56) that must be removed and replaced with `toast.error(extractApiError(err))`. All five files need `import { extractApiError } from '~/composables/useApi'` added — note this is a named import, not the `useApi()` composable call (which is separate).

**US-005 — LanguageSwitcher ref comparison (LanguageSwitcher.vue:31,42):** `locale` from `useI18n()` is a `Ref<string>`, not a string. Line 31 compares `locale === loc.code` (Ref vs string — always false), and line 42 binds `:model-value="locale"` (passes Ref object instead of string value). Must change to `locale.value === loc.code` and `:model-value="locale.value"` respectively. Note: `currentLocaleName` computed at line 12 already uses `locale.value` correctly and needs no change. `switchLocale` at line 18 already receives string codes and needs no change.

**Test approach:** The web app uses Jest. Component tests use Vue Test Utils. Existing tests live under `apps/web/test/` or co-located. All stories should write tests first.
